Seven courses of Mercury named (i) Prakrta, (ii) Vimi^ra,
(iii) Samksipta, (iv) Tiksn2, (v) Yoganta, (vi) Ghora and (vii)
Papa, have been mentioned in the Tantra (Scientific work) of Sage
Parasara, on the basis of the asterisms.

The course is known as PrakrtU when Mercury passes through
BharanI, Kcttika, RohinI or Svatl. It is Miira when the star is
Mrgasiras, Ardra, Aslesa or Magha. In the course called Samksipta
Mercury transits through Punarvasu, and the two Phalgunis. The
gait is called Ttk$na when his transit takes place through the two
Bhadrapadas, Jyestha, As vim and Revati.
